commit.message:
Convert multi-page site to single-page with scroll navigation

All section content (Education, Publications, Research, Notes, Gallery)
merged into index.html. Nav links now scroll to anchor sections instead
of navigating to separate pages. Header made sticky for persistent navigation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: remove server pubkey registry; look up recipient keys from blockchain

The GET /payment-info/{addr} endpoint and the server-side pubkey store
are removed. Senders now look up the recipient's compressed secp256k1
public key directly from the Stacks blockchain — any on-chain transaction
signed by an address reveals its public key (Hiro API:
GET /extended/v1/address/{addr}/transactions).

Server config (price, server address) still comes from GET /status.

Changes:
- app.ts: remove handlePaymentInfo and /payment-info route
- store.ts: remove savePublicKey/getPublicKey from interface + SQLite impl;
  remove public_keys table from migration
- auth.ts: remove savePublicKey calls; update comment
- client/types.ts: SendOptions.recipientPublicKey (required, caller-supplied)
- client/client.ts: send() fetches /status for server config instead of
  /payment-info; remove fetchPaymentInfo, add fetchServerConfig
- web UI: replace fetchRecipientInfo (server lookup) with lookupRecipientPubkey
  (Hiro API tx history); add TODO note for future BNSv2 zonefile lookup
- All tests updated; all 61 tests pass

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Skip to next photo after failed image retries are exhausted

Add MAX_RETRIES (2) to LayeredPhoto so it stops retrying a broken image
and calls onFailed. PhotoBackground advances to the next photo when
onFailed fires, preventing the slideshow from getting stuck on a broken
image. Extract advance() into a shared callback for both timer and
error-driven transitions.

commit.message:
feat(agents): add eval-informed failure warnings to all 9 agent prompts

Close the eval → prompt feedback loop (task 7.2). New module
`failure_warnings.py` reads `traces/analysis.json`, filters per-agent
criteria below 85% pass rate, and injects formatted warnings into
`build_system_prompt()` between L2 SKILL.md and L3 reference files.

- Mtime-cached to avoid re-reading JSON per call
- Max 5 warnings per agent, sorted worst-first
- Mock dry-run reasoning cleanup
- Graceful degradation when no analysis file exists
- 16 new tests (560 total passing)
- Phase 7 now fully complete (7.1–7.6)

perf: 3-4x faster embedding job — pipelining, larger batches, higher concurrency

- EMBEDDING_BATCH_SIZE: 10 → 40 (eliminate sequential sub-batching)
- Route batch sizes: 20→40 (images), 30→60 (text-only)
- Time budget: 180s → 240s (use 80% of maxDuration)
- Pipeline compute+write: overlap DB write with next Bedrock compute
- Add timing logs per batch for performance monitoring

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Reform package v3: decentralization-aligned polls + README

- Rewrote POLL 1.2 from identity/KYC mandate to on-chain accountability
  standards (wallet disclosure, voting records, pseudonymous profiles)
- Added Decentralization Rationale section to every poll (48 total)
  explaining how each proposal advances decentralization principles
- Updated POLL S.1 representative requirements: replaced 'verified unique
  identity' with Sybil-resistant on-chain proof (no personal identity required)
- Updated POLL 3.11 partnership due diligence: entity credentials and
  on-chain track record replace personal identity verification
- Updated POLL 1.3 ambassador elections: removed vetting requirements and
  mandatory re-election; eligibility via on-chain XCN holding only
- Updated POLL 2.8 ambassador term limits: removed mandatory re-election
  votes, kept term limits with community recall as accountability mechanism
- Updated POLL 2.10 compensation transparency: wallet-level disclosure,
  not named personal disclosure
- Updated POLL 1.1: reframed as codifying the existing 1M threshold
  (already in place) rather than lowering from 10M
- Fixed implementation timeline: replaced 'Leadership KYC deadline' with
  on-chain accountability disclosure deadline
- Added README.md with full package overview, decentralization principles,
  the three critical anomalies, and engagement guide

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: build initial PulseMap architecture (#1)

Implements the complete initial architecture for PulseMap — a serverless,
fat-client community health intelligence dashboard.

## What's included

**Core infrastructure**
- package.json with MapLibre GL, deck.gl, Vite, TypeScript strict mode
- tsconfig.json (ES2022, bundler moduleResolution, exactOptionalPropertyTypes)
- vite.config.ts with @/ alias and deck.gl/MapLibre chunk splitting
- index.html with dark theme (#0a0f1a) CSS variables

**Type system** (src/types/index.ts)
- HealthSignal, WastewaterData, AirQualityData, OutbreakAlert
- CommunityHealthScore, AnomalyAlert, AppState, FipsResult

**Vercel Edge Function API proxies** (api/)
- cdc-wastewater.ts → CDC NWSS Socrata endpoint (1hr cache)
- cdc-fluview.ts → CDC FluView ILI by HHS region (24hr cache)
- epa-airquality.ts → EPA AirNow observations (1hr cache)
- who-outbreaks.ts → WHO RSS feed with regex XML parser (30min cache)
- cms-hospitals.ts → CMS hospital data (24hr cache)
- nws-alerts.ts → NOAA NWS active alerts filtered to health types (5min cache)

**Data fetchers** (src/data/)
- fetchWastewater, fetchFluView, fetchAirQuality, fetchOutbreaks, fetchHospitals
- aggregator.ts: Promise.allSettled parallel fetch + freshness tracking
- All fetchers have mock data fallbacks for dev without API keys

**Scoring engine** (src/scoring/)
- communityRiskScore.ts: weighted composite 0-100 score (wastewater 30%,
  flu 25%, AQI 20%, hospitals 15%, outbreaks 10%)
- anomalyDetection.ts: Welford's online algorithm for streaming z-score
  anomaly detection (requires min 10 observations)

**Geo layer** (src/geo/)
- zipToFips.ts: Census Geocoding API with in-memory cache + ZipNotFoundError
- wastewaterLayer.ts: ScatterplotLayer with severity-based purple→red colors
- airQualityLayer.ts: ScatterplotLayer with EPA AQI official color scheme
- outbreakLayer.ts: Double-ring ScatterplotLayer for pulse effect

**UI components** (src/components/)
- MapView.ts: MapLibre GL + deck.gl MapboxOverlay, full viewport, hover tooltips
- Sidebar.ts: Health score widget + anomaly list + data source freshness
- ZipSearch.ts: 5-digit ZIP validation + Census lookup + search:zip event
- LayerControls.ts: Toggle buttons per layer with color dots + freshness times
- AlertBanner.ts: Slide-down critical alert strip with dismiss button

**Application wiring** (src/main.ts)
- AppState initialisation, component mounting, event bus (CustomEvent on document)
- map:ready → initial data load; search:zip → flyTo + reload; layer:toggle → re-render

**Documentation** (docs/)
- ARCHITECTURE.md: system design, data flow, scoring model, event bus
- DATA_SOURCES.md: all 7 data sources with URLs, fields, update frequency
- API_SETUP.md: zero-key quickstart, AirNow key setup, Vercel deploy guide

TypeScript strict mode passes with zero errors.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HU2TE6AS8r8rUuuy7Qs5mr

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: section conformité NIS2/RGPD dans le rapport PDF

Porte la logique de conformité de CompliancePage.tsx (frontend) vers
le backend, pour l'inclure dans tous les rapports PDF générés.

report_service.py :
- _build_compliance_context(findings, lang) — évalue les 12 critères
  NIS2/RGPD depuis les findings du scan (port Python de la logique TS)
  : 5 critères "essentiels" (HTTPS, TLS, DMARC, Headers, SPF)
  + 7 critères "avancés" (DKIM, DNSSEC/CAA, Ports, Réputation,
    Credentials, Expiry, Versions vulnérables)
- Chaque critère retourne pass/warn/fail/unknown avec label et couleur
- Score global (0–100), overall_status, pass/warn/fail counts
- _build_context() injecte "compliance" dans le contexte Jinja2

report_template.html :
- Nouvelle section "Conformité NIS2 & RGPD" insérée après le bloc
  "Ce qu'un pirate voit" (avant le Plan d'Action)
- Score de conformité global + tableau 12 critères avec statuts colorés
- Badges NIS2/RGPD par critère, article de référence
- Numérotation mise à jour : Plan d'Action +1, Annexes +1, CTA +1

tests/test_report_service.py :
- TestBuildComplianceContext : 11 nouveaux tests (127 total, 0 échec)
  DMARC p=none → warn, DKIM manquant → fail, CAA absent → warn,
  SSL expiré → fail, domain expiry → warn, réputation blacklist → fail,
  version vulnérable → fail, score 100% sans findings, langue EN

- project.json registers pillars under Planning group

commit.message:
Complete feature implementation and admin updates

✅ Updated admin authentication
- Removed demo login credentials
- Set new admin: taiwojos2@yahoo.com with secure password
- Updated seed_admin.js for database seeding

✅ Verified pricing system implementation
- Maximum $15 USD per kg for international routes
- Maximum ₦6000 NGN per kg for Nigeria/African routes
- African currencies auto-convert to NGN equivalent
- User-defined pricing per kg (replaces route-based pricing)
- Admin commission: 15% (configurable)

✅ Verified user experience features
- Landmark address displayed in search results
- Support page available at /support
- Wallet currency prompt after first trip post
- Currency selection modal with 7 major currencies

✅ Verified payment integrations
- Stripe Connect only shown for non-African currencies
- Naira bank account only shown for African currencies
- Stripe redirect to dashboard working correctly
- Auto-save Stripe verification status

✅ Form validation improvements

refactor(agents): extract BaseAgentService + standardise response schemas

Complete agent architecture improvements plan (.agents/plans/agent-improvements.md):

Phase 1 — BaseAgentService shared pipeline (app/ai/agents/base.py):
- 7 HTML transformer agents refactored, ~500 lines removed
- Thread-safe _get_model_tier + _should_run_qa hooks (fix singleton race conditions)
- Shared 8-step pipeline: prompt → LLM → post-process → QA

Phase 2 — Prompt gap fixes:
- Scaffolder SKILL.md: MSO namespace, accessibility, dark mode now MANDATORY
- Content SKILL.md: num_alternatives=1 enforcement

Phase 3 — Infrastructure wiring:
- Memory recall in blueprint engine _build_node_context()
- Confidence scoring on all agents via CONFIDENCE_INSTRUCTION
- Recovery router consumes handoff_history for cycle detection

Phase 4 — Recovery router bug fix:
- Remove "fallback" keyword collision (MSO vs personalisation)
- Add "merge tag" as personalisation-specific keyword

Phase 5 — Eval trace fixes:
- Dark mode traces now store input HTML (truncated to 5000 chars)
- Judge graceful degradation when input HTML missing

Phase 6 — Response schema standardisation:
- confidence + skills_loaded fields on all 9 agent response schemas
- to_handoff() method on BaseAgentService for standardised AgentHandoff

Also includes Knowledge agent (agent #8) with RAG pipeline, eval judge,
synthetic test data, and blueprint node.

544 tests pass.

Fix Apigee instance creation with dual peering ranges (#15)

* Fix SVG export: rename to .svg, export all diagrams

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix Apigee instance creation by using dual peering IP ranges

Instance creation was failing with NO_FREE_BLOCKS because the org
provisioning consumed all IPs in the single /22 range. Split into
two ranges: /20 for org peering and a separate /22 for the instance.
Also fixes idempotency — reruns now detect CREATING state instances.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix VPC peering step: add --force flag and connect-to-update fallback

The peering detection via `gcloud compute networks peerings list` can
miss existing peerings when the referenced address range was deleted.
Added --force to update command and a fallback from connect to update
when connect fails due to existing peering.

commit.message:
fix: remove reveal + transition classes from platform tab content

The reveal class (opacity: 0) requires IntersectionObserver which only
runs once on mount. Tab bar, feature cards, and trade example cards all
stayed invisible after tab switches because newly mounted DOM elements
were never observed. Removed reveal from all dynamic/interactive content
and removed transition-all from feature cards.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: hero background fades out on scroll, fades in on scroll back up

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

FPU-based RMSNorm: 3-kernel reader/compute/writer replacing scalar RISC-V (~119ms/tok)

Replace software scalar RMSNorm with Tensix FPU compute kernel using
hardware reduce, rsqrt, bcast_scalar multiply, and eltwise multiply.
Key findings: binary_op_init_common() required for packer init, output
CB must be c_16+, REDUCE_SCALAR applies scaler twice (use sqrt(1/N)).
~2.2x speedup from 262ms/tok to 119ms/tok.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: use BSD-safe sed for Tesseract patches, fix Ws2_32 in library

Replace range-based sed (BSD-incompatible brace/semicolons) with
line-matching deletes for removing the Tesseract CLI executable.

Also lowercase Ws2_32 -> ws2_32 globally in Tesseract's CMakeLists.txt
so the static library and its exported CMake targets use the correct
case for MinGW. This fixes both the CLI link and ensures FFmpeg can
link against libtesseract on Windows without case mismatch.
